description: The build uses dynamic version ranges or a floating sbt/Scala version, so it resolves differently across machines and over time.
body: Failure mode: build.sbt declares a dependency as latest.release or with a + range, or the sbt/Scala version floats, so coursier resolves different versions on different days or machines and a breaking release slips into CI or production unnoticed. Detection signals: latest.release/+ in libraryDependencies; no pinned version in project/build.properties; a build that worked yesterday fails today with no source change. Do not use dynamic version ranges in a build meant to be reproducible. Safer fallback: pin every dependency, plugin, sbt, and Scala version, verify a fresh checkout resolves an identical dependencyTree, and bump versions only through reviewed changes.
